2606.07433.title: Watch, Remember, Reason: Human-View Video Understanding with MLLMs
2606.07433.paper_summary: Video understanding is being rapidly transformed by multimodal large language models (MLLMs), as research moves from short clips to long, multimodal, and knowledge-intensive video scenarios. These scenarios require models to handle sparse evidence, long-range dependencies, multimodal alignment, and reliable inference under limited computational budgets. This work presents a human-view perspective on LLM-based video understanding, organized around three functional abilities: watching, remembering, and reasoning. Rather than treating video tasks as isolated benchmarks, this view provides a unified structure for analyzing how video MLLMs acquire evidence, preserve context, and produce grounded outputs. We introduce a formulation that characterizes video understanding systems by their perceptual representations, memory states, reasoning traces, and final predictions. Based on this formulation, we identify challenges in spatio-temporal perception, efficient long-video processing, memory modeling, streaming understanding, and faithful reasoning. Representative methods are organized by their roles in video MLLM systems. Watching covers fine-grained, comprehensive, audio-visual, and efficient perception. Remembering includes offline and streaming memory, while reasoning covers text-only reasoning and thinking with videos. We further examine application domains such as egocentric, sports, instructional, medical, and narrative videos, and cover training datasets and evaluation benchmarks across task types, supervision formats, modalities, and capability dimensions. Finally, we outline open problems and future directions for scalable, memory-aware, and evidence-grounded video intelligence. Related works will be continuously traced at https://github.com/marinero4972/Awesome-HumanView-VideoUnderstanding.
2606.07433.paper_summary_zh: 視頻理解正受到多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）的快速轉變，隨著研究從短片段轉向長篇、多模態和知識密集型的視頻場景。這些場景要求模型處理稀疏證據、長距離依賴、多模態對齊，以及在有限計算預算下進行可靠推斷。這項工作從人類視角出發，提出了基於LLM的視頻理解，圍繞三種功能能力組織：觀看、記憶和推理。這種觀點並不將視頻任務視為孤立的基準，而是提供了一個統一的框架，用於分析視頻MLLM如何獲取證據、保持上下文並產生有根據的輸出。我們介紹了一種表述，通過其感知表示、記憶狀態、推理痕跡和最終預測來描述視頻理解系統。基於這一表述，我們識別了在時空感知、高效長視頻處理、記憶建模、流式理解和忠實推理方面的挑戰。代表性的方法根據其在視頻MLLM系統中的角色進行組織。觀看涵蓋了細粒度、全面的音視覺和高效感知。記憶包括離線和流式記憶，而推理則涵蓋僅限文本的推理和與視頻的思考。我們進一步檢視了應用領域，如自我中心、體育、教學、醫療和敘事視頻，並涵蓋了跨任務類型、監督格式、模態和能力維度的訓練數據集和評估基準。最後，我們概述了可擴展的、具備記憶意識和基於證據的視頻智能的未解決問題和未來方向。相關工作將持續在 https://github.com/marinero4972/Awesome-HumanView-VideoUnderstanding 追踪。
2606.07433.author: Jiahao Meng et.al.
2606.07433.authors: Jiahao Meng, Yue Tan, Qi Xu, Kuan Gao, Weisong Liu, Yanwei Li, Jason Li, Lingdong Kong, Haochen Wang, Qianyu Zhou, Jiangning Zhang, Guangliang Cheng, Yunhai Tong, Lu Qi, Minghsuan Yang
